title: Experimental daemon-owned native Pi TUI worker lifecycle
goal: Add an opt-in native Pi TUI worker path that is launched and supervised by the Khazad-Doom daemon, preserves the JSON-wrapper path as default/fallback, and keeps all authoritative worker truth in KD-owned artifacts.
acceptance: The existing daemon JSON-wrapper Pi worker path remains default and remains fallback when the experimental TUI path is disabled or unavailable.
The experimental path is gated by an explicit opt-in and does not require Herdr or Pi TUI for headless/default worker execution.
The daemon launches the native Pi TUI through Herdr agent start semantics and passes a KD-owned result artifact path through environment variables.
The native Pi TUI worker result is accepted only from the submit_worker_result artifact source; terminal text, Herdr scrollback, Herdr metadata, and Pi display state are never correctness evidence.
Daemon-owned cancellation and worker-attempt timeout trip the supervised cancellation token and attempt to close the Herdr-hosted TUI pane.
The native TUI path returns the same worker ResultData shape as the wrapper path so existing validation, scope checks, gates, retry/repair, merge, and handoff lifecycle remain downstream.
Packaging policy is explicit: the worker extension is loaded per attempt from a daemon-prepared artifact directory and is not globally registered as a default Pi extension.
must_ask_if: The implementation would default-enable, replace, or remove the current JSON-wrapper worker path.
The implementation would treat terminal text, Herdr pane text, Herdr scrollback, Herdr agent state, or Pi TUI display state as authoritative worker result evidence.
The implementation would bypass daemon validation, scope checks, verification gates, repair budgets, cancellation, merge, or handoff state.
The implementation would make Herdr or a Pi UI extension required for headless/default worker execution.
The implementation would answer operator questions by typing into a worker pane instead of using KD daemon commands or ask_operator IPC.
